faqs.question: How do I know if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants in my Maspeth home?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than common household ants, typically measuring 1/4 to 1/2 inch long, and are usually black or dark brown. Unlike termites, carpenter ants don't eat wood but excavate it to create nesting galleries, leaving behind piles of sawdust-like wood shavings called frass. You may also hear faint rustling sounds inside walls or see large winged ants (swarmers) in spring, which indicate an established colony in or near your home.

faqs.question: Will one treatment eliminate my carpenter ant infestation?
faqs.answer: While some minor infestations can be resolved with a single treatment, most carpenter ant problems in Maspeth require 2-3 follow-up visits to ensure complete colony elimination. Carpenter ants often have satellite colonies in addition to the main nest, and it takes time for worker ants to carry treatments back to the queen. We include follow-up inspections in our service plans to guarantee long-term results and offer warranty protection for your peace of mind.
